skill.name: beads-workflow
skill.description: Converts markdown implementation plans into dependency-aware Beads tasks, then guides repeated review until tasks are ready for AI coding agents.
skill.summary: Turn markdown plans into polished, dependency-aware Beads tasks for AI coding workflows.
skill.author: Dicklesworthstone

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review shows the matches are markdown code fences, inline command names, and example prompts rather than executable Ruby backticks or weak cryptography. The skill documents local Beads CLI workflows and prompt templates; no evidence of network access, credential access, obfuscation, malicious intent, or prompt injection against the evaluator was found.

low_findings.title: Documented External CLI Commands
low_findings.description: The skill includes example commands for bd, bv, jq, and Agent Mail helper calls. These are workflow instructions for local project task management, not hidden execution code, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are explicit markdown examples of local CLI usage. They do not show dynamic command construction, network calls, secret access, or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Broad Local Context Review Prompts
low_findings.description: Several prompt templates ask an AI agent to read full project planning files such as AGENTS.md, README.md, and a markdown plan. This is expected for planning, but it increases local context exposure inside the active AI session.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompts clearly request broad local file review. No evidence shows exfiltration or credential harvesting, so the concern is limited to scope control.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance matches occur in prose and prompt examples, not in executable code. No evidence of cryptographic implementation, system probing, or malicious data collection was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows descriptive markdown and test-planning text. There is no code path that implements weak crypto or gathers host information.

content.user_title: Turn Plans Into Beads for Agent Workflows
content.value_statement: Large markdown plans are hard for AI agents to implement consistently. This skill turns plans into detailed Beads tasks with dependencies, review prompts, and readiness checks.
actual_capabilities: Provides prompts for converting markdown plans into Beads tasks and subtasks.
Explains how to add dependency structure between implementation tasks.
Defines review prompts for improving beads before implementation starts.
Documents bd and bv command examples for local Beads workflows.
Includes a checklist for task quality, test coverage, and dependency readiness.
Suggests cross-model review using Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.
limitations: Requires the Beads CLI and related tools to be installed separately.
Does not automate validation of bead quality or dependency graphs by itself.
Assumes the source markdown plan is accurate and complete.
Examples are focused on coding projects and may need adaptation for non-code work.

use_cases.title: Prepare a large implementation plan
use_cases.description: Convert a long markdown plan into task beads with background, scope, dependencies, and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Coordinate parallel AI agents
use_cases.description: Use dependency-aware beads so multiple coding agents can select ready work without losing project context.

use_cases.title: Improve task readiness before coding
use_cases.description: Run repeated review prompts to preserve features, add tests, and remove unclear work before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion of one markdown plan into Beads tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read PLAN.md and convert it into detailed Beads tasks with subtasks, dependencies, success criteria, and test coverage notes.

prompt_templates.title: Polish Existing Beads
prompt_templates.scenario: Review task quality before implementation begins.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all current beads for missing context, unclear dependencies, lost features, and weak test coverage. Update them using the bd tool.

prompt_templates.title: Check Implementation Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm that a task graph is ready for multiple agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the Beads graph for cycles, blockers, missing test tasks, and vague descriptions. Report changes needed before agents start work.

prompt_templates.title: Run Cross-Model Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review after several polishing rounds.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a second reviewer for the Beads plan. Compare task scope, dependencies, tests, and user impact. Recommend precise bead updates.

output_examples.input: A product plan for adding OAuth login, dashboard routing, and account settings.
output: A set of feature beads for authentication, protected routes, and account settings.
Dependency notes showing which data model and session tasks block later work.
Test beads covering unit, integration, and end-to-end login flows.

output_examples.input: An existing Beads list that has vague descriptions and missing dependencies.
output: Expanded task descriptions with background, success criteria, and implementation notes.
Explicit blocker relationships between setup, feature, and validation tasks.
A readiness summary showing remaining gaps before parallel agent execution.

output_examples.input: A team wants to split a release plan across several AI coding agents.
output: A prioritized workflow for selecting ready beads.
Coordination guidance for reserving files and reporting work by bead ID.
A final checklist for dependency health and test coverage.

best_practices: Start with a complete markdown plan before converting work into beads.
Review bead dependencies and test coverage before assigning work to agents.
Repeat polishing until new review passes produce only small changes.
anti_patterns: Do not create short task titles without background or success criteria.
Do not start parallel implementation while dependency cycles remain unresolved.
Do not drop planned features to make the bead graph look simpler.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps convert markdown implementation plans into detailed Beads tasks for AI coding workflows.

faq.question: Does it create beads automatically?
faq.answer: It provides prompts and workflow guidance. The user or agent still runs the Beads tools.

faq.question: Which tools does it assume?
faq.answer: It references bd for Beads management, bv for graph review, and Agent Mail for coordination.

faq.question: Can Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code style coding agents.

faq.question: Is this only for software projects?
faq.answer: The examples focus on software implementation, but the planning pattern can adapt to structured project work.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing beads?
faq.answer: Users should check scope, dependencies, missing tests, cycle risks, and whether each bead is self-contained.
